_metadata.description: Botanical extracts, herbs, and plant-derived ingredients with standardization markers and thresholds
_metadata.scoring_rule: Botanicals with min_threshold are scored higher when meeting standardization requirements. Priority level affects matching weight.
_metadata.pipeline_integration: Used by cleaning script to standardize botanical ingredient names and by enrichment script to identify quality markers

standardized_botanicals.notes: Antioxidant berry standardized for 1% anthocyanins. ORAC value is also used as quality marker.
Botanical ingredient standardized for fiber. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for saponins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for aloin, polysaccharides. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ginsenosides. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Indian gooseberry standardized for 40% tannins. Very high natural vitamin C content.
Immune support herb standardized for 10% andrographolides. AP-Bio standardized extract is common.
Cardiovascular herb standardized for 1% arjunolic acid and 16-20% tannins.
Digestive and liver support herb standardized for 2.5% cynarin and chlorogenic acids.
Adaptogenic herb standardized for withanolides. USP standard is 2.5% withanolides via gravimetric testing (equivalent to ~1.5% HPLC). Premium extracts like KSM-66 use 5%.
Botanical ingredient standardized for astaxanthin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for astaxanthin ≥ 3%, total carotenoids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Immune support herb standardized for 0.5% astragaloside IV or 16% polysaccharides.
Standardized botanical extract with polysaccharides, astragalosides as primary marker compounds.
Cognitive enhancement botanical standardized for bacosides. Range is 20-55% bacosides; clinical studies commonly use 300-450mg at 50% standardization.
Blood sugar support herb standardized for 1% corosolic acid (GlucoHelp standard is 1%).
Botanical ingredient standardized for vitamin C, polyphenols. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for chlorophyll. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for flavonoids, phenolics. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Performance vegetable standardized for 0.5-1% nitrates for cardiovascular benefits.
Blood sugar support alkaloid standardized to 97% berberine HCl. Emerging alternative to metformin in metabolic health supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for beta-glucans. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Eye health botanical standardized for 25% anthocyanins (anthocyanosides). Common in vision support supplements.
Blood sugar support herb standardized for 10% bitter principles (charantin).
Botanical ingredient standardized for triterpene glycosides, actein. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for anthocyanins, gamma-linolenic acid. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Bioavailability enhancer standardized for 95% piperine (BioPerine®). Added to turmeric and other supplements to enhance absorption.
Immune and anti-inflammatory oil standardized for 1-2% thymoquinone (TQ). Premium extracts like ThymoQuin use 3%.
Botanical ingredient standardized for sesamin, sesamolin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for fucoidan, iodine. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for phycocyanin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Antioxidant berry standardized for 1-3% anthocyanins.

aliases: Joint Shield 5-Loxin Advanced Bosclear Boswellia serrata extract
standardized_botanicals.notes: Anti-inflammatory herb standardized for 65% boswellic acids (AKBA is key marker). Some extracts go to 70%.
Cruciferous vegetable extract standardized for 0.4% sulforaphane. Used in detoxification and cancer prevention supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for arctiin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ruscogenin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Migraine prevention herb standardized for 7.5% petasins. Must be PA-free (pyrrolizidine alkaloid-free).
Botanical ingredient standardized for vitamin C. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for beta-carotene, alpha-carotene. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Joint and immune support herb standardized for 3% oxindole alkaloids (pentacyclic type preferred).
Botanical ingredient standardized for flavonoids, alkaloids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for phthalides, 3-n-butylphthalide. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for saponins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Immune support mushroom standardized for 8% beta-glucans or polysaccharides.
Botanical ingredient standardized for apigenin, bisabolol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Women's hormonal balance herb standardized for 0.5% agnuside (vitex agnus-castus).
Detox algae standardized for 3% chlorophyll. CGF (Chlorella Growth Factor) is key marker.
Botanical ingredient standardized for polyphenols, cinnamaldehyde. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Essential oil standardized for cinnamaldehyde, eugenol. Used for therapeutic and functional benefits.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ketosteroids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Polyphenol-rich bergamot extract used for lipid and cardiometabolic support. Clinical literature commonly profiles neoeriocitrin, naringin, and neohesperidin in bergamot polyphenolic fractions.

Cocoa extract used for vascular and cardiometabolic support. Clinical evidence most often references daily cocoa flavanol content and epicatechin-rich profiles rather than a universal label percentage threshold.
Botanical ingredient standardized for chlorogenic acids, polyphenols. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Weight management and cardiovascular herb standardized for 10% forskolin.
Performance mushroom standardized for cordycepin (0.1%+) or polysaccharides (40%+).
UTI prevention berry standardized for 36mg PACs per dose (A-type proanthocyanidins).
Botanical ingredient standardized for cucurbitacins, vitamin C. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Anti-inflammatory compound standardized for 95% curcuminoids. C3 Complex is the most studied form.
Botanical ingredient standardized for flavonoids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for sesquiterpenes, taraxasterol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Joint health herb standardized for 2% harpagosides. Clinical studies use 50-100mg harpagoside daily.
Women's health herb standardized for 1% ligustilide.
Immune support herb standardized for 4% echinacoside.
Standardized botanical extract with phenolic_compounds, echinacoside as primary marker compounds.
Immune support herb standardized for 4% phenolic compounds (cichoric acid, echinacoside).
Botanical ingredient standardized for flavonoids, phenolic acids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Immune support berry standardized for 10-15% anthocyanins. Sambucol and other extracts typically use 13% standardization.
Immune support berry extract standardized for 13% anthocyanins per Artemis International specifications.
Women's health oil standardized for 9-10% GLA (gamma-linolenic acid).
Blood sugar and testosterone support seed standardized for 50% saponins.
Migraine prevention herb standardized for 0.4% parthenolide.
Botanical ingredient standardized for lignans, alpha-linolenic acid. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Cardiovascular support botanical standardized for allicin or aged garlic extract (S-allylcysteine). Used in heart health and immune supplements.
Digestive and anti-nausea herb standardized for 5% gingerols. Higher concentrations (up to 20%) available.
Botanical ingredient standardized for 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Cognitive herb standardized for 24% flavone glycosides and 6% terpene lactones. EGb 761 is the most studied standardized extract.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ginkgolide A, ginkgolide B. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for polysaccharides, zeaxanthin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Immune support herb standardized for 5% hydrastine and 5% berberine.
Cognitive and wound-healing herb standardized for triterpenes (8-40%). TECA extracts contain 40% asiaticoside, 30% asiatic acid, 30% madecassic acid.
Cardiovascular antioxidant standardized for 90-95% OPCs (oligomeric proanthocyanidins).
Cardiovascular antioxidant standardized for 95% OPCs (oligomeric proanthocyanidins).
Botanical ingredient standardized for naringin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for acetogenins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Weight management extract standardized for 45-50% chlorogenic acids (GCA).
Antioxidant botanical standardized for 50% polyphenols and EGCG. Popular in weight management and metabolic health supplements.
Natural stimulant standardized for 22% caffeine (guaranine). Slower release than pure caffeine.
Blood sugar support herb standardized for 25% gymnemic acids.
Botanical ingredient standardized for gypenosides, saponins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Cardiovascular herb standardized for 1.8% vitexin and 18.75% oligomeric procyanidins.
Botanical ingredient standardized for stilbenes, TSG. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Holy basil (Tulsi) standardized for 2.5% ursolic acid. Also contains eugenol and rosmarinic acid.
Sleep support herb standardized for 4% alpha acids or xanthohumol content.
Men's health herb standardized for 10% icariin. Some extracts go to 20-60% icariin.
Botanical ingredient standardized for silica, flavonoids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for huperzine A. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Hibiscus (roselle) extract used for cardiometabolic support. Standardization is typically described by anthocyanin profile rather than a universal threshold percentage.
Botanical ingredient standardized for inulin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Nutrient-dense leafy green used in greens blends and superfood powders. Rich in vitamins A, C, K and antioxidants.
Botanical ingredient standardized for mesembrine. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for iodine. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for caffeine. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for puerarin, daidzein. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for L-theanine. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for linalool, linalyl acetate. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Calming herb standardized for 5% rosmarinic acid.
Botanical ingredient standardized for glycyrrhizin, glycyrrhizic acid. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for flavonoids, volatile oils. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Cognitive mushroom standardized for beta-glucans (10-40%) and nerve growth factors (hericenones, erinacines).
Adaptogenic root standardized for 0.6% macamides and macaenes. Gelatinized form improves absorption.
Stress and sleep support herb standardized for 2% honokiol and magnolol (total 90% in some extracts).
Immune support mushroom standardized for 20% beta-glucans (D-fraction is most studied).
Botanical ingredient standardized for mucilage, malvin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for anthocyanins, delphinidin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Eye health flower standardized for 5-20% lutein. Primary natural source of lutein/zeaxanthin.
Botanical ingredient standardized for mucilage, polysaccharides. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Liver support herb standardized for 80% silymarin complex. Silybin is the most bioactive component.
Botanical ingredient standardized for viscotoxin, lectins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for isothiocyanates, chlorogenic acids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Dopamine support herb standardized for 15% L-DOPA. Some extracts go to 98%.
Botanical ingredient standardized for DNJ (1-deoxynojirimycin). Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for verbascoside. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for erythrina alkaloids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Joint and prostate support herb. Root standardized for 1% beta-sitosterol; leaf for silica.
Botanical ingredient standardized for hydroxytyrosol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Cardiovascular and immune herb standardized for 18-20% oleuropein.
Standardized extract with 20% oleuropein. Some concentrates go to 40%.
Botanical ingredient standardized for quercetin, organosulfur compounds. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for carvacrol, thymol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Energy and cognitive support adaptogen standardized for 5% ginsenosides. Premium Korean Red Ginseng is highly valued.
Sleep and anxiety support herb standardized for 3.5% flavonoids (vitexin, isovitexin).
Botanical ingredient standardized for menthol, rosmarinic acid. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Essential oil standardized for menthol, menthone. Used for therapeutic and functional benefits.
Standardized botanical extract with procyanidins, catechins as primary marker compounds.
Botanical ingredient standardized for saponins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Cardiovascular and antioxidant fruit standardized for 40% ellagic acid or punicalagins.
Botanical ingredient standardized for betalains, indicaxanthin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for polyphenols, flavonoids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for fiber. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for OPC, procyanidins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Prostate health herb standardized for 13% total sterols including beta-sitosterol.
Antioxidant flavonoid standardized for 95% quercetin. Often paired with bromelain for absorption.
Women's health herb standardized for 8% isoflavones. Contains phytoestrogens.
Botanical ingredient standardized for catalpol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Immune and adaptogenic mushroom standardized for 10-40% polysaccharides or 4% triterpenes.
Antioxidant polyphenol standardized for 50% trans-resveratrol. High-potency extracts can reach 98%.
Adaptogenic herb standardized for rosavins (min 3%) and salidroside (min 1%). Traditional ratio is 3:1 rosavins:salidroside.
Botanical ingredient standardized for aspalathin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for galactolipids, vitamin C. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Antioxidant herb standardized for 4% rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid. Used as natural preservative and cognitive support ingredient.
Botanical ingredient standardized for crocin, safranal. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for tanshinones, salvianolic acid B. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for saponins, steroidal saponins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Prostate health herb standardized for 85-95% fatty acids and sterols per USP/European standards.
Adaptogenic berry standardized for 2% schisandrins (lignans).
Women's health adaptogen standardized for 40% saponins (shatavarin).
Immune support mushroom standardized for 10% beta-glucans or lentinan content.
Botanical ingredient standardized for 6-shogaol, 8-shogaol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for eleutherosides. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for mucilage. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for isoflavones, genistein. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Leafy green used in greens blends for lutein, zeaxanthin, and nitrates. Popular in pre-workout formulas for nitric oxide support.
Blue-green algae standardized for 15-25% phycocyanin content. Contains 60-70% protein.
Mood support botanical standardized for 0.3% hypericin. Used in mild-to-moderate depression and mood balance supplements. Interacts with many medications.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ecdysterones. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for anthocyanins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for thymol, carvacrol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Men's health herb standardized for 1-2% eurycomanone or expressed as concentration ratio (100:1, 200:1).
Men's health herb standardized for 45% saponins (protodioscin is key marker).
Immune support mushroom standardized for 30%+ polysaccharides (PSK, PSP).
Anti-inflammatory spice standardized for 95% curcuminoids when in extract form. Raw turmeric contains only 2-5% curcuminoids.
Botanical ingredient standardized for arbutin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Sleep support herb standardized for 0.8% valerenic acid per German Commission E recommendations.
Botanical ingredient standardized for chlorophyll. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Natural pain relief herb standardized for 15% salicin. Natural aspirin precursor.
Botanical ingredient standardized for diosgenin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for anthraquinones. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Energy herb containing 1-2% caffeine plus theobromine and chlorogenic acids.
Botanical ingredient standardized for saponins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.

African herb traditionally used for testosterone support. Limited clinical research - use with caution due to potential liver/kidney concerns.
Traditional Chinese medicine herb used for energy and vitality. Standardized for echinacoside content.
Himalayan mineral resin standardized for fulvic acid (50%+). Used in Ayurveda for energy and rejuvenation.
Berry rich in omega-7 fatty acids and antioxidants. Used for skin health and mucous membrane support.
Beauty mushroom known for skin-hydrating polysaccharides. Traditional use in Chinese medicine.
Medicinal mushroom with high beta-glucan content. Used for immune support.
Men's health herb often sold as crude extract (4:1 to 10:1 concentration ratio).
Prostate health seed standardized for 85% fatty acids when as oil extract.
Simple sugar used for urinary tract health. Not a botanical but commonly found in supplement formulas.
Phospholipid important for brain cell membranes. Supports cognitive function and stress response.
Pineapple enzyme standardized by activity (GDU/g or MCU/mg), NOT percentage. 2400 GDU/g is standard potency. 1 GDU = ~1.5 MCU.
South African plant extract (EPs 7630) clinically studied for respiratory health support.
Aromatic plant used for respiratory support. Contains eucalyptol (1,8-cineole).
Digestive herb containing anethole. Used for bloating and digestive comfort.
Digestive seed containing carvone. Often combined with peppermint for IBS support.
Extremely bitter herb used as digestive tonic. Stimulates stomach acid and bile production.
Bitter herb used for digestive and antimicrobial support. Use with caution due to thujone content.
High-antioxidant berry with exceptional anthocyanin content.
Red algae producing blue phycocyanin pigment. FDA approved May 2025 as natural food colorant.
African spice used for thermogenesis and metabolism. Contains 6-paradol which activates brown fat.
Ayurvedic herb (Curculigo orchioides) used for vitality and testosterone support.
Ayurvedic herb traditionally used for libido, oral health, and nerve stimulation.
Berry rich in anthocyanins and ellagic acid. High antioxidant capacity.
Branded grape seed extract standardized for cognitive health benefits.
Patented coffee fruit extract shown to increase BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor).
Patented potato protein extract that promotes satiety by triggering CCK release.
